entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp. targeted for protein enhancement through breeding.

entities.name: PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The level of protein in the seeds, which was increased from 20-22% to 28-30% in derived lines.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL PLANT BREEDING
entities.description: The breeding tools used to transfer the high-protein trait from wild relatives to cultivated types.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES OF PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The non-cultivated species of pigeonpea used as the source for the high-protein trait.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, measured in kg/ha.

entities.name: HPL 40
entities.description: A high-protein inbred line of pigeonpea producing 2100 kg/ha grain yield.

entities.name: HPL 8
entities.description: A high-protein inbred line of pigeonpea producing 1660 kg/ha grain yield.

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring human beings have access to vital nutrients like protein.

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: The general category of protein-rich plants that includes pigeonpea.
